title: American buttercream
subtitle: Crusting buttercream ratio and method for frosting cakes
excerpt: Butter and icing sugar beaten to a stiff, sweet frosting that crusts dry on the surface within 20 minutes. The ratio is 1:2 butter to icing sugar. Used for piped rosettes, smooth sides, and cupcake frosting.
sourceNotes: Butter-and-icing-sugar frosting is a domestic confection documented from the early twentieth century in British and American domestic cookery. The term 'American buttercream' distinguishes this raw-sugar version from Swiss meringue, Italian, and French buttercreams (all of which use cooked sugar or egg). The crusting characteristic (the surface sets dry) is specific to the high icing-sugar ratio and is used in viva cake decorating to smooth the set surface with a paper towel.

content.text: American buttercream is defined by its ratio: one part softened butter to two parts icing sugar by weight, beaten until pale and fluffy. A small amount of milk or cream adjusts the consistency. The high sugar content means the frosting develops a dry crust within 20-30 minutes of application, which allows it to be smoothed further with a paper towel once set, a technique called viva smoothing. Below the crust, the interior stays soft.

The butter must be at room temperature before beating. Cold butter produces a lumpy, curdled-looking frosting that does not come together with more beating. If your kitchen is cold, cut the butter into pieces and leave it for an hour before starting.
Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} alone in a stand mixer on medium-high for 3-4 minutes until pale and noticeably fluffy. This aeration is what makes the finished frosting light rather than dense.

Add the {{icing-sugar}} in three additions, beating on low between each addition to prevent a cloud of sugar. Once all the sugar is incorporated, add the {{vanilla-extract}}, {{fine-salt}}, and {{whole-milk}} and beat on medium-high for 2 minutes until fluffy and smooth. The frosting should hold a peak but spread easily. Add more milk a teaspoon at a time for a softer spreading consistency; add a little more icing sugar to stiffen for piping.

recipe.yieldDescription: frosts one 20 cm 3-layer cake or 24 cupcakes
